source_jobs.title: Director, Compensation
source_jobs.description:
UTA is seeking a strategic and experienced Director of Compensation to join our dynamic People team onsite in our Beverly Hills office. In this role, you will lead the design, implementation, and management of our global compensation programs, ensuring they are competitive, equitable, scalable and aligned with business goals. You will own the end-to-end annual compensation cycle, including merit, bonus, and other incentives programs, while partnering closely with HR, Finance, and senior leadership to drive pay-for-performance strategies.



This role also includes building frameworks for job architecture, salary structures, incentive programs, analyzing market data, and advising on pay equity and total rewards strategy. You will be a trusted advisor to senior leadership, contributing to both tactical execution and long-term compensation strategy. This position reports to the SVP of Total Rewards, People Systems & Operations and plays a critical role in shaping UTA’s total rewards philosophy and programs.



The salary range for this role is $190,000 - $225,000 commensurate with experience and skills.



What You Will Do

 - Lead day-to-day compensation operations across our diverse business units, including market pricing, role leveling, career ladder development, and FLSA compliance reviews.

 - Direct the annual merit and bonus planning process, including timeline development, system configuration, communications, and analysis of outcomes.

 - Conduct ongoing market intelligence and industry benchmarking to ensure pay competitiveness, inform program enhancements, and maintain best-in-class compensation practices.

 - Apply compensation philosophy and principles to design, implement, and maintain key compensation programs.

 - Collaborate with Senior Leadership and the People Team to develop, pilot, and implement innovative compensation programs that support current and future business objectives.

 - Lead or support cross-People Team initiatives and projects, ensuring alignment with broader People and business strategies.

 - Partner with People Systems & Data Management teams on compensation systems development, implementation, and ongoing maintenance, driving data accuracy and usability.

 - Work closely with stakeholders across People, Legal, Finance, and business leadership to continuously improve compensation processes with a focus on efficiency, quality, and scalability.

 - Support the SVP of Total Rewards, People Systems & HR Operations in shaping the overall compensation function, including team development and process enhancements, ensuring alignment with company strategy and culture.

What You Will Need

 - 10+ years of compensation experience, including 5+ years of direct management and leading project teams.

 - Prior consulting experience is a plus.

 - Strong analytical, modeling, and data-driven skills, with HCM experience (Workday preferred) and survey aggregators (CompAnalyst preferred). Experience with Workday Advanced Compensation module is also a plus.

 - Bachelor’s degree in HR, Finance, Business, or related field; CCP or advanced degree preferred.

 - Deep expertise in base and variable compensation, including managing large-scale merit and bonus cycles across diverse business units.

 - Proven track record designing and rolling out compensation programs, job architectures, and salary structures that drive business results.

 - Hands-on experience with market benchmarking, pay equity, and incentive program design, using insights to shape strategy and inform leadership decisions.

 - Exceptional stakeholder management and communication skills — able to influence executives and translate complex concepts into clear, actionable guidance.

 - Skilled in broad project management and process optimization, driving efficiency, scalability, and consistency across global teams.

 - Business-savvy, with a solid understanding of financial implications and budget impact of compensation programs.

source_jobs.title: Associate, Advisory
source_jobs.description:
UTA Advisory seeks an Associate to join our team as part of our Consultancy function. In the exciting role as an Associate, you will lead market research, situation analysis through quantitative and qualitative means and contribute your perspective on how our clients learn, grow and transform in their respective sectors of media, entertainment, marketing and technology.

As an Associate at UTA Advisory, you will apply analytical thinking and experience-based judgment to complex challenges by focusing on the critical issues, opportunities and constraints that impact business performance. Associates are most often staffed across multiple clients or engagements, supporting UTA Advisory teams actively as they advise clients, develop strategic insights and implement key recommendations.



The salary range for this role is $70,000 to $80,000 commensurate with experience and skills.

 

What You Will Do

 

Research and Analysis
● Conduct market and company analyses, strategic diligence, and competitive benchmarking on key business issues in marketing, media, advertising, and technology
● Evaluate corporate & business unit strategies, reflecting market, competitive and key external drivers
● Assess new businesses and revenue opportunities and lead the development and execution of market sizing, business and investment cases, capability assessments and change management plans

 

Client Service & Project Management
● Drive day to day project management (both internal and external) across several client engagements or workstreams
● Action follow-ups for timetable of tasks to reach scope of work objectives and owns calendars, trackers, agendas and notetaking
● Monitor weekly to-do's and take proactive measures to set the team and engagement up for success
● Support the creation and development of client deliverables based on guidance of engagement lead such as marketplace analysis, client benchmarking and stakeholder discovery & analysis

 

Communication & Collaboration
● Communicate effectively across the project team to ensure internal and external messaging is aligned
● Ensure proper and timely escalation of project risk or potential for missed delivery deadlines
● Work with internal teams to integrate learnings and best practices from related projects
● Play an active role in proposals, thought leadership efforts, and other market and business development activities

 

What You Will Need
● 2+ years of work experience in consulting, media, marketing, advertising and/or technology
● Proven analytical skills, expertise in qualitative and quantitative research
● Fluency in media/advertising/technology industry terms and trends
● Excellent interpersonal skills; strong oral and written communication skills
● Strong PowerPoint and Excel skills are essential
● Strong project management and organizational skills
● Impeccable attention to detail

What You Will Get



 - The unique and exciting opportunity to work at one of a leading global entertainment companies

 - Access to the tools, leadership, and resources you will need to create and drive a center of excellence

 - The opportunity to do the best work of your career

 - Work in an inclusive and diverse company culture

 - Competitive programs to support your well-being

 - Experience working in a collaborative environment with room to grow

This executive must be an organized, strategic and skilled practitioner who thrives in a high-volume practice that supports a large group of agents.



The salary range for this role is $130,000 to $250,000 commensurate with experience and skills.

 - Take ownership of ad-hoc projects, demonstrating flexibility, initiative, and problem-solving skills.

source_jobs.title: Vice President of Data and Technology Solutions
source_jobs.description:
UTA seeks a Vice President to join our advisory team, leading Data & Technology Solutions (DTS) for some of the world’s largest brands, agencies, and media organizations.

 

Our DTS practice serves as a strategic partner and subject matter expert across UTA Advisory’s Transformation and Growth Strategy practices. We specialize in marketing technology, data, identity, and measurement, helping clients modernize their organizations and achieve future-proofed growth from evolving data-driven capabilities and cutting-edge business models through data and intelligence.

 

We work with brands, agencies, media companies, and investors to navigate the rapidly evolving marketing landscape, advising on everything from ‘mad tech stack’ strategy to AI-driven operating models to measurement frameworks to data privacy and identity resolution. Our expertise enables clients to place data at the center of their marketing and business strategy and unlock competitive advantage through innovation.

 

Equal parts strategic advisor, marketing technologist, and client service leader, you will play an integral role in helping our clients assess, design, and implement best-in-class data and technology solutions. Reporting to a Principal, Vice Presidents define strategic approaches, manage stakeholder relationships, and drive execution of key deliverables. You will also contribute to business development, helping to expand UTA Advisory’s DTS offering and industry presence.

 

Vice Presidents may be staffed across multiple engagements, supporting CMOs, CTOs, CIOs, and data/technology leaders in navigating the challenges of modern marketing. This is an ideal role for a leader with deep technology expertise, a passion for solving complex business challenges, and an ability to bridge the gap between strategy and execution.

 

The salary range for this role is $200,000 to $250,000 commensurate with skills and experience.

source_jobs.description:

We are expanding our team to meet growing global demand, and we are seeking a Director with deep industry knowledge and strategic insight to drive growth and deliver exceptional contract advisory services.



In this role, you will act as a trusted advisor to leading advertisers across industries—including CPG, Auto, Retail, Pharma, FinServ and Entertainment—optimizing agreements representing billions of dollars in annual media buying and marketing value through agency partners. You will leverage analytical rigor, legal expertise, and strategic judgment to identify risks, uncover opportunities, and drive enhanced performance across complex, multi-channel contracts.



You will support the Principal, Head of Global Contract Advisory to drive industry thought leadership and empower client organizations to navigate the evolving commercial landscape shaped by emerging technologies, regulatory changes, and industry transformation. The ideal candidate will demonstrate exceptional communication, precision, and analytical thinking, applying experience across data governance, omni-channel media, and global agency partnerships to address critical business challenges, future opportunities, and legal constraints affecting brand performance. Additionally, you will play a key role in new business development, helping to expand client relationships, identify growth opportunities, and bring C.O.R.E.’s advisory services to new markets and partners.



You are a strategic thinker and trusted advisor who thrives in complex, fast-paced environments. You combine legal expertise with business acumen, bringing both precision and pragmatism to contract challenges. You communicate effectively across organizational levels, build strong client relationships, and take initiative in identifying new opportunities and innovations.



Your judgment is sound, your work is meticulous, and you have the credibility to guide negotiations and resolve disputes. You stay current with industry trends from regulatory and compliance shifts to emerging technologies like artificial intelligence and you can translate these developments into practical contract strategy for clients. You are eager to grow with MediaLink and contribute to our expanding global platform while developing thought leadership that shapes industry conversations.

source_jobs.description:

In this role, you will design and implement security solutions across physical, virtual, and cloud environments. By leveraging your expertise in cybersecurity and knowledge of common attack vectors, you will enhance visibility, threat correlation, and incident response capabilities throughout our technology landscape. As a key contributor to our cloud‑first strategy, you will play a critical role in shaping and advancing our overall security posture.

 - Investigate and contextualize security events from numerous data sources

 - Help detect, respond to, and remediate security events and incidents

 - Develop remediation and orchestration efficiencies across the security stack, including endpoint, network, identity, and application

 - Perform e‑discovery tasks in support of Legal and HR investigations

 - Respond to user requests for security‑related issues and concerns

 - Improve current visibility by configuring existing logging and alerting policies

 - Be given the opportunity to rapidly accelerate security, technology, and management skills

 - Have the freedom to experiment with novel problem‑solving approaches

 - Be exposed to emerging security and business technologies

 - Have access to best‑in‑class tooling and people

 - Grow and learn on the job every day

 - Maintain awareness of trends in various security domains

 - Collaborate with other teams to improve security posture, risk remediation, and threat awareness

 - Bachelor’s degree in Cybersecurity, Engineering, or a related field; or equivalent experience

 - Experience with incident response, security event triage, investigations, forensics, and fluency with endpoint operating systems (Windows/Mac/Linux) and command line tools

The role will also be responsible for developing and monitoring key business performance metrics related to revenue, operating expenses, and capital spending, among other areas, to help facilitate financial and strategic decision-making throughout the organization. source_jobs.description:
UTA seeks a VP, Learning & Development to report to UTA’s SVP, People & Head of Employee Experience. This individual will lead the evolution of Learning & Development into a scalable, global, outcomes-focused function that builds manager and leadership capability across the enterprise. This role is accountable for driving excellence in learning, coaching, and performance enablement by designing and governing end-to-end development experiences that are standardized where it matters, tailored where it counts, and measurable. This role does not include a relocation package.



The salary range for this role is $220,000 to $240,000 commensurate with experience and skills.



This leader will oversee core learning offerings, including the effectiveness of UTA’s LMS, manager and leadership development, and professional skills, with a strong emphasis on content design, practical application, and continuous improvement. The role partners closely with People Business Partners and business leaders to ensure learning and coaching solutions are efficient, future-ready, and supported by simple, effective processes and tools that meaningfully improve outcomes.

 - Build and lead a high-performing global Learning & Development team grounded in collaboration, accountability, and exceptional service to employees and leaders.

 - Provide strategic and operational leadership to ensure learning, coaching, and performance enablement programs are scalable, efficient, and employee-centric.

 - Partner with HRBPs, Talent Acquisition, and business leaders to align learning priorities with workforce plans, succession needs, and organizational change initiatives.

source_jobs.title: Senior Network & Systems Engineer
source_jobs.description:
UTA seeks a Senior Network & Infrastructure Engineer to join our Technology team. As a key member of the department, your primary mission will be to architect, optimize, and maintain the critical infrastructure that connects our 12 global offices. You will ensure seamless, high-availability, and secure inter-office and cloud-edge connectivity for our global workforce.

You will also play a pivotal role in managing our cloud-edge integrations (AWS/Azure) and overseeing core systems such as Identity, Email, servers and cloud environments.

 

The salary range for this role is $140,000 to $150,000 commensurate with experience and skills.

 

What You Will Do

 

 - Global Office Connectivity Infrastructure: Oversee, optimize, and maintain stable network connectivity across 12 global offices. Manage the full physical and logical office network, including Cisco Meraki configuration, High Availability (HA) WAN topologies, wireless connectivity, UPS systems, rack patching, low-voltage cabling coordination, access control, and IP camera connectivity

 - Cloud & Hybrid Networking: Architect, deploy, and maintain secure network infrastructure within and between AWS and Azure environments, ensuring robust and redundant cloud-to-prem connectivity

 - Modern Identity & Governance: Administer cloud-native identity management, lifecycle workflows, and governance strictly via Microsoft Entra ID. Design, implement, and enforce granular Conditional Access policies

 - Infrastructure Management & Patching: Manage, monitor, and maintain Windows and Linux server infrastructure across cloud and on-prem environments. Lead server lifecycle management and patch orchestration using Azure Update Manager (Azure Updates)

 - Business Continuity & Disaster Recovery (BC/DR): Design, implement, test, and maintain robust infrastructure backup and disaster recovery strategies. Ensure continuous data protection and system availability using Azure Backup and Azure Site Recovery (ASR) alongside on-premises backup solutions

 - Enterprise Email & SaaS: Administer Microsoft 365 with a heavy emphasis on Exchange Online architecture, email security/hygiene, mail flow routing, and core collaboration platforms

 - Monitoring & Observability: Design and implement comprehensive monitoring, logging, and alerting systems across all network, cloud, and server infrastructure to ensure uptime and performance

 - Automation & Infrastructure as Code: Automate routine operations, server provisioning, and maintenance tasks using PowerShell, Bash, and Python to improve reliability and reduce manual effort

 - Tier 3 Escalation: Provide advanced troubleshooting and act as the final escalation point for complex cloud infrastructure, physical/virtual servers, physical network and global Wi-Fi diagnostics, and identity issues

 - Security & Documentation: Partner with Security to implement preventative controls and support incident response. Define, document, and enforce infrastructure standards, SOPs, and architectural diagrams

What You Will Need

 

 - 8+ years in Systems/Network Engineering with proven experience managing multi-site, global office network connectivity. Strong hands-on experience with Cisco Meraki stack, HA WAN environments, and physical Wi-Fi troubleshooting

 - Solid understanding of physical data room components, including low-voltage cabling, patch panels, UPS maintenance, and IoT/security devices (cameras, access control)

 - Strong experience with AWS and Azure cloud networking architectures and secure hybrid routing

 - Deep, production-level expertise with Microsoft Entra ID, Conditional Access, and identity governance in a pure-cloud identity environment (no legacy Active Directory or Group Policy)

 - Proven experience administration and provisioning of Windows and Linux server environments, with specific experience managing updates via Azure Update Manager

 - Hands-on experience designing and executing backup and disaster recovery workflows using Azure

source_jobs.title: General Assistant
source_jobs.description:
UTA is seeking a General Assistant to support across the Music department in our London office.

 

Reporting into the Music Operations Manager, you’ll be responsible for administrative tasks including ticket counts, guest list management, immigration requirements for touring artists, and working closely with the Finance department on tracking payments for shows. You’ll provide desk cover when Agents’ Assistants are on leave, and will receive all-round training in the administrative responsibilities of an Assistant.

 

What You’ll Do:

Your responsibilities will include, but not be limited to:

• Scheduling meetings – organising meetings with promoters

• Managing roster grids – keeping London office roster information up to date

• Managing contact lists – keeping documents such as All Agents List up to date

• Finance – prepare weekly outstanding payment reports, and working with agents, assistants, and the finance team to track and chase deposits and final balance payments

• Updating show lists – compiling London show list on a weekly basis

• Enquiries – dealing with enquiries as they come in via email or phone • Ticket figures – checking ticket figures and sending to management

• Managing guest list – managing festival and headline show guest list requests • Support ideas – collating internal and external support suggestions, including for other agencies when requested

• Event assistance – helping organise UTA presence at events such as IFF and TGE

• Contracts – contracting shows quickly, accurately and efficiently

• Immigration – filing for immigration forms, including Certificates of Sponsorship for UK bands, and generally assisting management with immigration needs and managing documentation for touring parties

• Meeting minutes – taking notes on calls as and when requested

• Desk cover – providing desk cover when Agents’ Assistants are on leave

• Support –providing daily ad hoc support to agents and assistants, when approved by Operations Manager

 

What You’ll Need:

• A passion for working in the music industry

• Excellent verbal and written communication skills

• Excellent organizational skills

• Ability to prioritize a busy workload

• Ability to develop business relationships

• Previous experience in a related field is desirable

What You Will Get



 - The unique and exciting opportunity to work at one of a leading global entertainment companies

 - Access to the tools, leadership, and resources you will need to create and drive a center of excellence

 - The opportunity to do the best work of your career

 - Work in an inclusive and diverse company culture

 - Competitive programs to support your well-being

 - Experience working in a collaborative environment with room to grow



About UTA


UTA is a premier global talent agency built for the future of media and entertainment. The agency's diversified platform and best-in-class, client-first approach is innovative, collaborative, and positioned to lead in an evolving market. UTA represents the most celebrated artists, creatives, and brands, from icons and legends to next-generation talent. Its integrated capabilities span film and television, music, comedy, creators, sports, brands, news, publishing, speakers, theater, and more. It is based in Los Angeles with offices in the U.S., London, and Munich.

For more information: https://www.unitedtalent.com/about/

UTA and its Affiliated Companies are Equal Employment Opportunity employers and welcome all job seekers.
